modules.title: Transformer‑I
topics: Separation of hysteresis and eddy current losses
Tests: open circuit and short circuit, load, Sumpner’s test
Condition for maximum efficiency and regulation

topics: Three‑phase transformer: construction, groups and connections, working and applications

topics: Comparison of slip‑ring and squirrel‑cage motors

modules.title: Three‑phase Induction Motor‑II
topics: Starting of squirrel‑cage and slip‑ring motors

modules.title: Single‑Phase Motors
topics: Single‑phase induction motor: double revolving field theory, equivalent circuit and its determination, performance calculation
Starting methods and types of single‑phase induction motors: working principle and applications

experiments: Perform turn ratio and polarity test on 1‑phase transformer
Perform load test on a 1‑phase transformer and plot its load characteristic
Perform OC and SC tests on a 1‑phase transformer and determine its equivalent circuit; also find its efficiency and regulation at different loads and power factors
Perform OC and SC tests on a 3‑phase transformer and determine its equivalent circuit; also find its efficiency and regulation at different loads and power factors
Perform Sumpner’s test on two 1‑phase transformers and determine its efficiency at various loads
Perform no‑load and block‑rotor test on a 3‑phase induction motor and determine its equivalent circuit
Perform load test on a 3‑phase induction motor and plot its performance characteristics
Study various types of starters used for 3‑phase induction motors
Perform no‑load and block‑rotor test on a 1‑phase induction motor and determine its equivalent circuit

modules.title: Number Systems and Codes
topics: Digital number systems, base conversion, binary, decimal, octal, hexadecimal, number system with radix r, Gray codes
Alphanumeric codes – ASCII code and BCD codes, concept of parity, complement r's & (r‑1)'s, subtraction with complements, signed binary numbers, error detecting & correcting codes
Basic theorems and properties of Boolean algebra: AND, OR, NOT operators, Boolean algebra laws, De Morgan’s theorem, Boolean expression & logic diagram
Negative logic, alternate logic‑gate representation (bubbled gates), canonical and standard forms (minterms & maxterms), sum‑of‑minterms and product‑of‑maxterms conversion
Truth tables and Karnaugh maps for 2‑, 3‑, 4‑, 5‑ and 6‑variable functions, solving digital problems using maps, don’t‑care conditions, tabular minimisation
Sum‑of‑products and product‑of‑sums reduction, exclusive‑OR & exclusive‑NOR circuits, parity generator & checker design

modules.title: Combinational Circuits
topics: Half‑adder, full‑adder, half‑subtractor, full‑subtractor and code‑converter designs
Analysis of design using universal building blocks
Implementation of any logic function using only NAND gates or only NOR gates
Binary serial adder, parallel adder, serial/parallel adder, look‑ahead carry generator, BCD adder
Binary multiplier and magnitude comparator designs
Decoder, demultiplexer, encoder, priority encoder implementations
Multiplexer design and implementation of combinational logic diagrams

modules.title: Sequential Logic Circuits
topics: Latches: SR latch (NAND & NOR), D latch, edge‑triggered flip‑flops, JK flip‑flop, T flip‑flop, master‑slave flip‑flop
Analysis of clocked sequential circuits: state tables, state diagrams, state reduction, state equations, state assignments
Flip‑flop excitation tables and characteristic equations
Design procedure for sequential circuits using state reduction
Applications of flip‑flops in sequential logic design

modules.title: Registers and Counters
topics: Asynchronous and synchronous counters, modulus counters, down counters, up/down counters, ripple‑counter propagation delay
Programmable counters, presettable counters, BCD counters, cascading counters, counter applications and decoding glitches
Ring counter, Johnson counter, rotate‑left and rotate‑right counters
Registers: buffer register, shift‑left, shift‑right, bidirectional shift registers, parallel‑in‑parallel‑out, serial‑in‑serial‑out, parallel‑in‑serial‑out, serial‑in‑parallel‑out registers

modules.title: Memory and Converters
topics: Random Access Memory (RAM): timing waveform, memory decoding, internal construction, coincident decoding, address multiplexing
Read‑Only Memory (ROM) implementation, types of ROMs, combinational PLDs, Programmable Logic Array (PLA), Programmable Array Logic (PAL), sequential programmable devices
Analog‑to‑Digital conversion techniques: ramp, dual‑slope, integrating, successive‑approximation, parallel, serial, converter specifications
Digital‑to‑Analog conversion techniques: binary‑weighted and R/2R DACs

experiments: Verification of all the logic gates
Design of BCD to Excess‑3 code converter
Implementation of NAND & NOR as universal gates
Design of RS, JK, T & D flip‑flops

Design of various A‑D & D‑A converters
Verification of the truth table of SR flip‑flop

Introduction to conventional, non-conventional & distributed generation
